items.q: Which energy system fuels a marathon?
items.why: Students associate hard exercise with anaerobic systems. Long-duration steady-state work uses AEROBIC metabolism (oxygen + carbs/fats) - efficient but slower to ramp up. Anaerobic systems dominate brief intense efforts.

items.correct: Volume of blood pumped by the heart per minute (stroke volume x heart rate).
items.misconception: The volume of blood in the heart at any moment.
items.why: Students confuse stroke volume (one beat) with cardiac output (per minute total). CO = SV x HR. A 70mL stroke at 70bpm = 4.9L/min cardiac output.

items.q: How long does the ATP-PC system last at maximum intensity?
items.why: Students overestimate the ATP-PC system. It provides instant energy but stores deplete in ~10 seconds (one sprint, one heavy lift). After that, glycolysis or aerobic systems take over.
Correct answer: Work in pairs. “Work alone” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Biceps and triceps move which joint?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Elbow. “Knee” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many main muscles bend or straighten the elbow (biceps + triceps)?
items.why: The real figure is 2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
